q: An optical instrument used for viewing ___ objects is called a telescope.
Two types of telescope are ___ and terrestrial telescope.
The refracting astronomical telescope consists of an objective of ___ and an eyepiece of short focal length.
The final image formed by the eyepiece in an astronomical telescope is virtual and ___ with respect to the distant object.
To obtain the sharp images of distant objects the objective should have a large ___.
The ___ was first suggested by Newton.
It is not convenient to use the astronomical telescope for viewing objects on the earth since the final image produced is ___.
In the terrestrial telescope a ___ lens, instead of a convex lens, is used as an eyepiece to produce a final image which is erect.
Total reflecting prisms are used in ___ to reduce the length of the terrestrial telescope.
Binoculars consist of a pair of ___ one for each eye.
